library.description: ملحق SDL3 الرسمي للصور والرسوم المتحركة وتحويلها إلى موارد قابلة للاستهلاك.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

identity.name: IMG_Load_IO
identity.titleArabic: تحميل الصورة SDL data source software السطح

summary.actualTranslationArabic: تحميل الصورة SDL data source software السطح
summary.actualMeaningArabic: تحميل الصورة SDL data source software السطح.
summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى تحميل الصورة SDL data source software السطح داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic: القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه (SDL_Surface *) Returns a new SDL surface, or NULL on error. وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.
summary.sourceDescription: Load an image from an SDL data source into a software surface.

parameters.name: src
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل src من النوع SDL_IOStream * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription: an SDL_IOStream that data will be read from.

parameters.name: closeio
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل closeio من النوع bool القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription:
true to close/free the SDL_IOStream before returning, false to leave
it open.

remarks:
An SDL_Surface is a buffer of pixels in memory accessible by the CPU.
Use this if you plan to hand the data to something else or manipulate it
further in code.

There are no guarantees about what format the new SDL_Surface data
will be; in many cases, SDL_image will attempt to supply a surface that
exactly matches the provided image, but in others it might have to
convert (either because the image is in a format that SDL doesn't
directly support or because it's compressed data that could reasonably
uncompress to various formats and SDL_image had to pick one). You can
inspect an SDL_Surface for its specifics, and use SDL_ConvertSurface to
then migrate to any supported format.

If the image format supports a transparent pixel, SDL will set the
colorkey for the surface. You can enable RLE acceleration on the surface
afterwards by calling: SDL_SetSurfaceColorKey(image, SDL_RLEACCEL,
image->format->colorkey);

If closeio is true, src will be closed
before returning, whether this function succeeds or not. SDL_image reads
everything it needs from src during this call in any
case.

There is a separate function to read files from disk without having
to deal with SDL_IOStream: IMG_Load("filename.jpg") will
call this function and manage those details for you, determining the
file type from the filename's extension.

There is also IMG_LoadTyped_IO (),
which is equivalent to this function except a file extension (like
"BMP", "JPG", etc) can be specified, in case SDL_image cannot autodetect
the file format.

If you are using SDL's 2D rendering API, there is an equivalent call
to load images directly into an SDL_Texture for use by the GPU without
using a software surface: call IMG_LoadTexture_IO () instead.

When done with the returned surface, the app should dispose of it
with a call to SDL_DestroySurface().
details.returns: (SDL_Surface *) Returns a new SDL surface, or NULL on error.
